briefs.title: Restricted Insurance Agent Licence rules take effect January 2027 for BC businesses selling insurance
briefs.summary: Starting January 1, 2027, BC businesses that sell certain types of insurance alongside their main products — such as car dealers selling gap insurance, farm equipment dealers selling warranty insurance, or funeral providers selling prepaid funeral insurance — must hold a Restricted Insurance Agent Licence (a limited permit allowing non-insurance businesses to sell specific insurance products). This regulation defines exactly which insurance types qualify and who must be licensed.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business sells any insurance products and apply for a Restricted Insurance Agent Licence before January 1, 2027.

key_dates.label: BC Restricted Insurance Agent Licence Regulation takes effect
